title: (比周星馳悲觀) 吳孟達: 香港希望渺茫, 年輕人可嘗試往外闖

replies.msg:
吳孟達: 香港希望渺茫, 年輕人可嘗試往外闖

http://www.sohu.com/a/286544578_563941

這個世界這裡沒路了就往外衝

許知遠：好多人懷念 70 年代、80 年代的香港，好多人懷念所謂的香港精神，你現在覺得香港精神到底是什麼？

吳孟達：說真話，我們懷念是因為那個時候我們比較容易滿足，比較踏實，願意工作，不管你做什麼，哪怕你在外面撿垃圾你都能活。當時有很多工作，洗碗或者出工，你都能養活自己，甚至富裕一點去養一個小家庭，現在就好像很渺茫——看不到希望人們就開始迷茫，這個也是一個危機，因為你再怎麼樣算，也沒有什麼機會能讓香港的房地產緩和下來，它只會往上，地方真的就那麼大。你叫那些年輕人怎麼辦？他們在香港長大、受教育，可出來後卻沒有找到理想的工作。即便找到理想的工作，距離買房子也太遙遠了，現在香港隨便一個房子上千萬，那要讓他們打工打多久？

許知遠：您經歷過這麼多事情，您對這些年輕人有什麼建議嗎？

吳孟達：往外面想想，這個世界這裡沒路了就往外衝，因為你了解香港之後，你就知道機會更多的是留給一些更需要在香港活的人，而你要面對太多跟你同樣情況的競爭。你如果覺得自己有才華，那就往外闖，外面很多片天，你要試啊。你不爬出這個第一步，你哪知道第二步的精彩，你沒試過，你只會跟著大潮流，人家說怎麼樣，你就跟著人家起哄，你永遠不能滿足的。
此回覆已被刪除

周星馳對香港好樂觀

http://news.mingpao.com/ins/%E6%B8%AF%E8%81%9E/article/20170729/s00001/1501320005156

2017年7月29日星期六

【短片】訪解放軍夏令營　周星馳：更多年輕人參加，香港更有希望 (18:20)

《中國日報》微博前天（27日）發放一段長約1分14秒片段，內容是香港演員周星馳日前到解放軍駐港部隊夏令營訪問，其間穿插他點讚這個夏令營活動，指「假如香港有更多的年輕人能夠去參加這個夏令營，我覺得香港就更有希望」。

片段中既有周星馳的訪問，也有夏令營學員的受訓場面。周星馳在訪問中表示，「其實我覺得想感謝他們來到這裏。其實我應該跟他們說的，但是剛剛沒有機會，就是因為他們把時間起碼拿來參加這個夏令營活動」。

周星馳續說：「因為這裏面是一個比較刻苦的訓練，還有就是體驗啦等等，這個都是比較嚴厲的，比較要受苦的，但是他們肯花時間去體驗一個刻苦的東西，而不是拿去玩，我覺得是很難得。我真的覺得是要感謝他們。」他又說，「還有我覺得假如香港有更多的年輕人能夠去參加這個夏令營，我覺得香港就更有希望。」

片段最後部分，周星馳獲安排跟部分青少年學員在一個室內環境分列前後排，跟他們一同做「心心」手勢。此前亦有穿插部分學員在戶外操練的片段。

quote.msg:
其實大陸大城市都幾多機會，不過法治情況好嚴重
如果好似IT呢類人才適應到大陸環境，我覺得仲好過係香港打個幾萬蚊工

replies.msg:
If you can really do IT then move to the Bay Area / San Francisco. 20x Hong Kong’s pay, 100x the opportunities.

Google’s entry level hire for top IT students last year was 200k usd + bonus. That’s 16萬港幣一個月 and you are 23歲。Plus you can go anywhere after 3-5 years at google.

That’s just one sample of many - i tell all my friends and family - if you are good at tech / IT goto USA. In 10 years time you will have an infinitely better future than any of your banker/doctor/government worker friends in HK could ever dream of.

Ps. We are in contact with a lot of the hiring of these USA grads. For every one software developer grad there are about 5-7 job offers waiting for them. Hk is a pathetic village stuck in a time warp from 30 years ago.

咪多左年青人用工餘課餘時間向外闖

what about non-it grads/career?

replies.msg:
Depends. What is your major? USA is very result oriented and these days all the cost efficiency breakthroughs and new product areas require advanced tech, science or math knowledge. They are not joking when they say they really care about STEM education.

If it’s just social sciences / liberal arts etc then I am sorry - probably better luck in SE Asia.

All my friends in the advanced sciences / tech in the States have done way better than I and any of my local network has done here in HK - and we are all investment bankers / professionals with OK paying jobs etc. There is a reason why USA is as advanced as they are in the world. Different world.

quote.msg: what about non-it grads/career?

Depends. What is your major? USA is very result oriented and these days all the cost efficiency breakthroughs and new product areas require advanced tech, science or math knowledge. They are not joking when they say they really care about STEM education.

If it’s just social sciences / liberal arts etc then I am sorry - probably better luck in SE Asia.

All my friends in the advanced sciences / tech in the States have done way better than I and any of my local network has done here in HK - and we are all investment bankers / professionals with OK paying jobs etc. There is a reason why USA is as advanced as they are in the world. Different world.

Econ&fin.  However i didnt take a traditional route like my classmates did.  I worked in law firms for a few years after graduation and recently joined the legal team of a chinese investment bank.  I am not a lawyer but from what i have seen laywers in HK arent well-paid these days.  That‘s why i am planning on a career change.  My situation is a bit  complicated...haha sorry

replies.msg:
Going to be tricky - Econ and finance degree is too ‘soft’ and without a proper internship / previous work experience you won’t stand out.

Chinese legal team is sort of a black mark - we know how they function with the legal side of things. Don’t think that’s going to help you at all with western companies in the states sorry.

You can try to go for an advanced degree in hard sciences or quants at a top University but not sure how good your skills are. All dependent on your own ability.

But your current track won’t make you very appealing as a candidate in the long run, I am sorry to say.

replies.msg: 未來呢?

吳孟達: 香港希望渺茫, 年輕人可嘗試往外闖

http://www.sohu.com/a/286544578_563941

這個世界這裡沒路了就往外衝

許知遠：好多人懷念 70 年代、80 年代的香港，好多人懷念所謂的香港精神，你現在覺得香港精神到底是什麼？

吳孟達：說真話，我們懷念是因為那個時候我們比較容易滿足，比較踏實，願意工作，不管你做什麼，哪怕你在外面撿垃圾你都能活。當時有很多工作，洗碗或者出工，你都能養活自己，甚至富裕一點去養一個小家庭，現在就好像很渺茫——看不到希望人們就開始迷茫，這個也是一個危機，因為你再怎麼樣算，也沒有什麼機會能讓香港的房地產緩和下來，它只會往上，地方真的就那麼大。你叫那些年輕人怎麼辦？他們在香港長大、受教育，可出來後卻沒有找到理想的工作。即便找到理想的工作，距離買房子也太遙遠了，現在香港隨便一個房子上千萬，那要讓他們打工打多久？

許知遠：您經歷過這麼多事情，您對這些年輕人有什麼建議嗎？

吳孟達：往外面想想，這個世界這裡沒路了就往外衝，因為你了解香港之後，你就知道機會更多的是留給一些更需要在香港活的人，而你要面對太多跟你同樣情況的競爭。你如果覺得自己有才華，那就往外闖，外面很多片天，你要試啊。你不爬出這個第一步，你哪知道第二步的精彩，你沒試過，你只會跟著大潮流，人家說怎麼樣，你就跟著人家起哄，你永遠不能滿足的。


你如果覺得自己有才華，那就往外闖，外面很多片天，
你要試啊。你不爬出這個第一步，你哪知道第二步的精彩，你沒試過，你只會跟著大潮流，人家說怎麼樣，你就跟著人家起哄，你永遠不能滿足的

quote.msg:
周星馳對香港好樂觀

http://news.mingpao.com/ins/%E6%B8%AF%E8%81%9E/article/20170729/s00001/1501320005156

2017年7月29日星期六

【短片】訪解放軍夏令營　周星馳：更多年輕人參加，香港更有希望 (18:20)

《中國日報》微博前天（27日）發放一段長約1分14秒片段，內容是香港演員周星馳日前到解放軍駐港部隊夏令營訪問，其間穿插他點讚這個夏令營活動，指「假如香港有更多的年輕人能夠去參加這個夏令營，我覺得香港就更有希望」。

片段中既有周星馳的訪問，也有夏令營學員的受訓場面。周星馳在訪問中表示，「其實我覺得想感謝他們來到這裏。其實我應該跟他們說的，但是剛剛沒有機會，就是因為他們把時間起碼拿來參加這個夏令營活動」。

周星馳續說：「因為這裏面是一個比較刻苦的訓練，還有就是體驗啦等等，這個都是比較嚴厲的，比較要受苦的，但是他們肯花時間去體驗一個刻苦的東西，而不是拿去玩，我覺得是很難得。我真的覺得是要感謝他們。」他又說，「還有我覺得假如香港有更多的年輕人能夠去參加這個夏令營，我覺得香港就更有希望。」

片段最後部分，周星馳獲安排跟部分青少年學員在一個室內環境分列前後排，跟他們一同做「心心」手勢。此前亦有穿插部分學員在戶外操練的片段。

If you can really do IT then move to the Bay Area / San Francisco. 20x Hong Kong’s pay, 100x the opportunities.

Google’s entry level hire for top IT students last year was 200k usd + bonus. That’s 16萬港幣一個月 and you are 23歲。Plus you can go anywhere after 3-5 years at google.

That’s just one sample of many - i tell all my friends and family - if you are good at tech / IT goto USA. In 10 years time you will have an infinitely better future than any of your banker/doctor/government worker friends in HK could ever dream of.

Ps. We are in contact with a lot of the hiring of these USA grads. For every one software developer grad there are about 5-7 job offers waiting for them. Hk is a pathetic village stuck in a time warp from 30 years ago.

quote.msg:
Depends. What is your major? USA is very result oriented and these days all the cost efficiency breakthroughs and new product areas require advanced tech, science or math knowledge. They are not joking when they say they really care about STEM education.

If it’s just social sciences / liberal arts etc then I am sorry - probably better luck in SE Asia.

All my friends in the advanced sciences / tech in the States have done way better than I and any of my local network has done here in HK - and we are all investment bankers / professionals with OK paying jobs etc. There is a reason why USA is as advanced as they are in the world. Different world.

Econ&fin.  However i didnt take a traditional route like my classmates did.  I worked in law firms for a few years after graduation and recently joined the legal team of a chinese investment bank.  I am not a lawyer but from what i have seen laywers in HK arent well-paid these days.  That‘s why i am planning on a career change.  My situation is a bit  complicated...haha sorry

Going to be tricky - Econ and finance degree is too ‘soft’ and without a proper internship / previous work experience you won’t stand out.

Chinese legal team is sort of a black mark - we know how they function with the legal side of things. Don’t think that’s going to help you at all with western companies in the states sorry.

You can try to go for an advanced degree in hard sciences or quants at a top University but not sure how good your skills are. All dependent on your own ability.

But your current track won’t make you very appealing as a candidate in the long run, I am sorry to say.

replies.msg: What are your views on computer Remote Sensing and geo-data technician? (GIS - geographic information system)
